title: fagaceae
intro: The Fagaceae rank is a taxonomic family of flowering plants, commonly known as the beech family. This family comprises approximately 600 species, including beech trees, oak trees, and related species. Fagaceae plants are typically deciduous or evergreen trees or shrubs, often found in temperate and subtropical regions of the world. They are characterized by their alternate or opposite leaves and acorn-like fruits, which are a distinctive feature of many species within this family.
